steps.title: Group matching terms
steps.content:
Group the four terms into two pairs. The first two terms share a factor involving $x^2$, and the last two terms share a factor involving $y^2$. This sets up a common binomial factor.
$$10x^3+40x^2y+xy^2+4y^3$$
$$=(10x^3+40x^2y)+(xy^2+4y^3)$$

steps.title: Pull out common factors
steps.content:
Factor each group separately. The first group has common factor $10x^2$, and the second group has common factor $y^2$.
$$10x^2(x+4y)+y^2(x+4y)$$

steps.title: Use the shared factor
steps.content:
Both terms now share the factor $x+4y$, so factor it out. The other factor is made from the remaining pieces, $10x^2$ and $y^2$.
$$(10x^2+y^2)(x+4y)$$
The answer is B.
